stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Iznik in northwest Turkey was a major center of ceramic production from the fifteenth to the seventeenth centuries.
  • Iznik ceramics are known for their distinctive decorative techniques.
  • Two key techniques are underglaze and overglaze painting.
  • In the underglaze technique, designs are painted directly on the unfired clay before a clear glaze is applied.
  • In the overglaze technique, designs are painted on top of an already-fired glaze and then re-fired at a lower temperature.
stem: The student wants to contrast the two ceramic-decoration techniques. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: In the underglaze technique, designs are painted on unfired clay before a clear glaze is applied, whereas in the overglaze technique, designs are painted on top of an already-fired glaze and then re-fired.
Among the famous Iznik ceramics are pieces decorated using the underglaze technique, in which designs are painted on the unfired clay.
Featuring both underglaze and overglaze decoration, Iznik ceramics are known for their distinctive techniques.
Ceramics decorated using underglaze and overglaze techniques can be found in Iznik, Turkey.

rationale: Choice A is the best answer because it directly contrasts the two techniques, describing how each one is performed. Choice B is incorrect because it describes only the underglaze technique. Choice C is incorrect because it mentions both techniques without contrasting them. Choice D is incorrect because it locates the techniques without contrasting them.
